title: On the Nature of Luck
author: From: Seneca
content: Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity. You cannot create the opportunity, but you can be so prepared that when it arrives, it is inevitable.

title: The Law of Triviality (Refined)
author: From: C. Northcote Parkinson
content: The trivial occupies the time that the important lacks. By ignoring the trivial, you reclaim your most valuable time.

title: The Sunk Cost Fallacy
author: From: Economics
content: Don't throw good time after bad. The cost is already spent; future returns are the only thing that matters.

title: The Observer Effect
author: From: Physics
content: The act of paying attention to a process changes the process. When you focus on a metric, the metric often improves simply because it is being watched.

title: The First Principles Approach
author: From: Aristotle
content: The first principle of any task is to ask: 'What is the absolute necessity here?' Anything beyond that is design, not function.
